content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Found 2 robustness issues in the planned-elsewhere flow: explicit-id aliasing can make valid mapped beads look stale, and comment delivery is effectively at-most-once with no retry after transient failures. Targeted `cargo test planned_elsewhere --quiet` passed.

## Amendments

1. Handle explicit-id aliases when validating and verifying `mapped_to_bead_id`. The prompt builder canonicalizes explicit bead IDs to `milestone_id.short_id` in [src/cli/project.rs:1179](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs#L1179), `extract_pe_bead_ids` and final-review validation only accept that exact token from the prompt in [src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:277](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs#L277) and [src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:648](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs#L648), and `verify_bead_exists` later passes the stored value straight to `br show` in [src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:70](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs#L70). For explicit beads that still exist in `.beads` under their authored short ID, a valid planned-elsewhere mapping will be treated as stale and its comment will never post. Resolve both the qualified and short-ID forms here; the alias requirement is already acknowledged in [src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:494](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs#L494).
   Rationale: This is a real correctness bug for explicit-id beads: valid mappings can be downgraded to stale warnings solely because the prompt/rendered ID and the BR-owned ID use different aliases.
2. Separate comment-delivery state from `mapped_bead_verified`, or defer marking a mapping verified until the comment has been durably flushed. `verify_mappings` skips every `mapped_bead_verified=true` row in [src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:277](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs#L277), but `verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success` persists that verified row before attempting `br comments add` and `sync_flush` in [src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:798](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs#L798) and [src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:832](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs#L832). Because `PlannedElsewhereMapping` has no field tracking whether the comment actually landed in [src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:736](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs#L736), any transient comment or flush failure becomes permanent: later replays skip the mapping entirely and never retry the optional audit comment.
   Rationale: The implementation is robust for verification replay but not for comment replay. A single transient BR failure can permanently drop the future-implementer context that this bead is supposed to surface on the mapped-to bead.
